words.tip: Short and punchy.
Also logical connector: 'als... dan...' = if... then...
Van + dag (of + day). Soft 'g' at end.
Context tells you which. 'Morgenochtend' = tomorrow morning.
Soft 'g' at start.
'ijd' = 'ayd' sound.
'ooi' = 'oy' sound.
Very commonly used.
Plural: de minuten. 'Vijf minuten' = five minutes.
Long 'uu'. 'Twee uur' = two o'clock / two hours.
Een kwartier = 15 minutes. 'Kwart over twee' = quarter past two.
Elke dag = every day. Plural: de dagen.
Deze week = this week. Plural: de weken.
Final 'd' → 't'. Plural: de maanden.
Plural: de jaren.
's Ochtends = in the morning (adverb form).
's Middags = in the afternoon. 'Goedemiddag' uses this.
's Avonds = in the evening. 'v' → 'f' sound.
Soft 'ch' at end. 's Nachts = at night.
Hoe laat is het? = What time is it? Op de klok = on the clock.
French origin — 'zh' sound like in 'measure'.
French origin. Plural: de seizoenen.
Soft 'g'. 'In het begin' = in the beginning.
Na (after) + middag. The PM half of the afternoon (~14:00-18:00). 'In de namiddag drink ik koffie.'
Adverb form — used with verbs. 'apostrophe + s' is an old genitive. ''s Morgens drink ik koffie.'
Synonym of ''s morgens'. Note apostrophe BEFORE the s, not after: 's ochtends, not s'ochtends.
''s Middags eet ik een boterham.' = In the afternoon I eat a sandwich.
''s Avonds kijken wij naar het journaal.' = In the evening we watch the news.
''s Nachts slaap ik diep.' = At night I sleep deeply. Covers midnight–6am.
Van + avond. 'Vanavond koken we Mexicaans.' Triggers inversion.
Used for THIS morning specifically. 'Vanmorgen heb ik koffie gedronken.'
Used without article in idioms: 'om middernacht' = at midnight.
